findings.title: Whitney accent-mobility rules are machine-encodable
Homonym token-splitting has a hard morphological ceiling
The Warnemyr scrape union-smears homonym classes
PWG nominal grammar compresses into 335 paradigm tokens
Whitney self-contradicts on derivative ī-stem gen.pl accent
Whitney accent axis validates at 17/19 matrix cells GO against attested RV accents
vidyut dhātupāṭha adjudicates the 2014 Palsule-exclusion dispute: five añc dhātus, no and, but ast IS Paninian
The parallel corpus rarely attests prefixed-verb forms
No printed frequency dictionary of Sanskrit exists
DCS lemma data is keyed in two transliterations
Unaccented DCS cannot distinguish present class I from VI
DCS OccId and sent_id are not unique keys
DCS UD tense marking conflates aorist and perfect
DCS 2021 and 2026 vintages are not directly comparable
A fifth of DCS lemmas have no CDSL headword
Sa-Ru glossary token coverage plateaus at 86.6 percent
Renou period-state tagging covers 770k entries in 8 dicts
Varga distribution is almost epoch-stable (Cramér's V = 0.037) — and the Gasūns-2014 dissertation prose read its own χ² table backwards
6.6 % of the DeepSeek corpus word-alignments ground to nothing in their verse
PWG encodes secondary stems inline, not in div markup
Giant verb roots sit at non-zero homonym indexes
PWG orders senses genetically, not historically
Vedic-citation density separates the dictionary traditions
SKD and VCP carry essentially zero Western markup
The ls source map recognises 72.4 percent of PWG citations
PWG citation occurrences track distinct references
MW has no sense-level div markup
Apte is three dictionaries; keys differ stem vs nominative
About 9 percent of typo corrections are collisions
A verified correction queue decays against live csl-orig
Citation density is register-bound, not comparable raw
Sense granularity is a family trait, not a diachronic trend
MW inherited the PWG apparatus skeleton, not its prose
PWG and MW share 94,753 headwords in the union index
Body-text headword mining is a dead end (38.6 percent precision)
Detector precision stratifies by digitization quality
Correction events concentrate in sense text
Twelve years of corrections cover only ~10–14 % of the estimated error population
PW-only headwords outnumber PWG-only ones 6-to-1 — PWG is not the sole spine of the local layer universe
The MW→WordNet→semdom bridge is a candidate generator, not a classifier
PWG's own closing sense-marker glyph "〉" was never recognized by the sense-splitter — ~50% of German senses were silently merged into their first sub-sense
csl-atlas's PWG parse-rules census is stale and incomplete — 21 real markup tags missing, several listed counts wrong
PWG's sense-closing glyph "〉" nests FOUR enumeration tiers, not two — Greek letters and roman-numeral markers are unrecognised by the RU pipeline's splitter
The pwg_ru RU store's `h` field has inconsistent semantics — not a reliable homograph-number join key
PWG `<ls>` citation resolution is already at 98%+, far above the previously-cited 72.4% baseline
Indigenous dictionaries agree on derivation; Wilson is the outlier
The E abbreviation tag is polysemous across dicts
Root-recovery tiers err on root form, not identity
IAST Unicode collides and normalises lossily
`nfold` earns sandhi-tolerant recall by fusing every nasal to `n` — which manufactures false quotation matches unless every hit is re-verified on `norm`
DCS `sentence.text_sandhied` is not reliably sandhied — some rows store analyzed word forms, which silently downgrades verbatim quotations to partial matches
BOM state is inconsistent across exports
Injected BOMs crash the hw record parser
devanagari_to_slp1 mis-routes retroflex la
Gloss-language spelling drift tracks reform type, not age
Practical Russian transcription of Sanskrit names has no safe reverse transliteration
The Sanskrit dictionary-platform landscape, probed live
Heritage data is acquirable despite the Anubis wall — via a GitHub mirror; the morphology XML is not in it
SKD/VCP sense/citation fusion is a record-type effect, not a dictionary-level one
Raw Latin-string tallies over gloss text include etymological false positives; Bopp lacks √yabh
Siglum prefix-families routinely bundle several distinct works; the diacritic-stripping fold has poisoned keys
VedaWeb 2.0's resource export is an async task behind a pickup-key, not a direct GET — and the server went unresponsive mid-attempt
MW↔Heritage coverage highlighting is a duplicate-anchor pattern, not a CSS class — and the mirror's "current" dictionary is a different-scope asset than the 2014 reader stem list
CDSL display paths are NOT uniformly `/2020/web/` — and two new digitizations landed in June 2026
CORRECTED — the MWScan/2020 `servepdf.php` endpoint is RIGHT (serves 1899); the 1872 first-edition scan coexists on the portal with colliding page numbers
Huet correspondence predates this session (2021) — the morphology-XML "gate" was already resolved in writing; direct download URLs recovered
Heritage vs kosha forms diff: the small raw overlap is mostly convention + model difference, and "disagreements" are two-thirds lemmatization policy, not error
The WIL etymology extraction's affix field is ~half noise — Wilson "outlier" figures are substantially a measurement artifact
DICO's entry anchors nest three structural roles under one HTML class — only one is a true entry boundary
`gen_opt_harness2.py` output-budget: coarser wins on both knobs, in opposite directions
samskrtam.ru/z/ is id-addressed with no name lookup — deep-linking needs a scraped root→id table; 8 primer-basic roots are absent
PWG-RU promoted store has input-level provenance, but old RU rows lacked exact model versions
Böhtlingk's *Indische Sprüche* (both editions) already fully digitized in `sanskrit-lexicon-scans`, not just `sanskrit-lexicon`
pwg_ru TM composite grade: A is consensus-gated (5.7%), and a reference-free surface QE cannot detect wrong-sense
The reverse dictionary's 30 sources split ~18 PD vs ~10 in-copyright — the merged headword list is not automatically publishable
PWG marks case government explicitly ≈3,853 times across ≈3,222 senses — a deterministic census, not an estimate
VedaWeb's `id_gra` token field IS the Grassmann `<L>` entry number — no fuzzy text-matching needed for a GRA↔VedaWeb crosswalk
VedaWeb 2.0's "CC BY 4.0 for everything" claim is not machine-confirmed — only 2/36 catalog resources carry an explicit license field
The ls-graph citation matrix is degenerate for MW — its top abbreviations sit unresolved; use the citation-apparatus siglum matrix for cross-dict citation profiles
The DCS `QL` frequency workbook's `SLP1` and length columns are truncated at ṣṭh/ḍh clusters
In PWG, article size dwarfs every "parametric" statistic you can extract from the entry
The Sanskrit spellchecker landscape: one dormant demo, one license-unsettled 543k wordlist, no occupant
Hand-transcribed telemetry cannot adjudicate code-vs-infra — and a local-only ledger silently swaps your denominator
The full Devībhāgavata-purāṇa Sanskrit is NOT on GRETIL — only the Devigita fragment; the complete mūla lives on sanskritdocuments.org without `DbhP_` markers
DCS 2026 sqlite carries 531,747 sense-annotated tokens (`m_wordsem`) but NO local ID→gloss inventory — gold-scored WSD against MW senses is blocked until the inventory is recovered
Amarakosha and SIL semdom both bolt a formal annex onto a semantic taxonomy — and it is the same ~10% once polysemy is set aside
DCS 2021→2026 "lost lemma" counts are mostly lemmatization-policy drift — a-privatives now resolve to their bases
DCS `text_sandhied` is largely DE-sandhied pada text in the Rāmāyaṇa — and locus joins fail across editions; a text-keyed 3-tier match (exact / consonant-skeleton / fuzzy) recovers it
vidyut-cheda 0.4 lemmatizes derivatives to the dhātu ROOT (rāmaḥ → ram) where DCS uses the nominal stem — and over-segments epic verse 1.44×
MW `<e>` encodes the 1899 print's headword typography (1 = Devanāgarī entry, 2 = roman-only, 3 = run-on compound; letter suffix = continuation record)
MW and the Petersburg dictionaries are NOT independent witnesses on inventory or apparatus — do not count their agreement as corroboration; but no shared *error* has ever been found
pwg_ru readiness audit: `[NWS:]` attribution and `{%…%}`-delimiter dropping are NOT audit-contract defects; observed token/cost economy is `not_recoverable`; store-membership ≠ audit-clean
The DCS snapshot's UD dependency slice is real but VEDIC-SKEWED — syntax studies get counterexample hunts, not classical norms
A curated DCS text→period map EXISTS (consume, don't rebuild) — and the purāṇas carry a measured epic-imitative signature on two independent axes
DCS verbal-feature annotation density collapses for later texts — feats-based diachronic metrics measure ANNOTATION, not language
A clean-looking subset is not promotable evidence when its audit or execution contract failed
Samāsa-type frequency does not exist in any org corpus — and the grammarians' canonical examples are corpus-ghosts (8/58 attested, max freq 147)
The roadmap's "OBS-T κ=0.42" was a phantom figure — no measured agreement exists for any OBS-T axis

MW writes `<ls>` citations in TWO markup shapes and locates them in roman as well as arabic — a literal `<ls>` regex undercounts its apparatus by 28.6%, and case-folding the roman test erases the `L.` hedge
A spelling-keyed join onto Whitney's roots union-smears homonyms — one authorial entry lands on every homonym of that spelling, and the rows still read `authorial`
DCS has no aorist TENSE value — `feat_tense='Past'` lumps aorist with the perfect; `feat_formation` is what actually separates them
A verified claim register is not Whitney-proof — 3 of 229 erdict_fact: TRUE rows in Kochergina claims.yml contradict Whitney, and ~65 of the register/article §-refs point at the wrong section
Declared, validated, and never enforced — the PWG headless executor read a manifest `budgets{}` block it did not obey, and every offline gate stayed green
kosha's generated `forms` is 93% DCS-derived, so its attested-form join is a round-trip — only the vidyut-engine subtotal (12.4% attested) carries signal, and A¬G cannot measure engine gaps
DharmaMitra `unsandhied` batches return MISALIGNED results on short inputs — doubled echoes and other texts' tokens — so every consumer must validate by surface reconstruction before display
SamudraManthanam's generated full-corpus JSONL has 38,288 duplicate canonical-ID groups, concentrated in `devibhagavata-purana`

Cross-dictionary attestation via Monier-Williams overstates independence — MW was compiled *from* Böhtlingk-Roth (PW/PWG), so an MW-only hit is not evidence a PW/PWG word is independently text-attested
Quantified: the §83/§97 witness-collapse deflates the published 15-dict union "corroboration" from 55.9% to 34.7% — and the union's own table is pre-fold

PD's inline sigla contain a near-homograph pair that similarity-clustering silently fuses — `MahāBhā.` is the Mahābhārata, `MahāBh.` is the Mahābhāṣya, and the locator shape tells them apart mechanically
Output gates must audit structured semantic fields, and sample-clean editorial rewrites still require a full-population ambiguity pass
DCS's compound dictionary carries splits whose member **order** does not match the surface form — invisible to a type-drill, fatal to any head-first analysis
`10.5281/zenodo.15834721` is a false DOI, cited as genuine in two different repos
The DCS `dcs-conllu` treebank is only ~3.9 % dependency-parsed — corpus government/valency work must lean on co-occurrence, not arcs, and read absence as "unknown"

MW's derivation markup and the DCS corpus are productive over the *same* compound final members but with near-disjoint first members (median Jaccard 0.00, 56% share zero) — and the corpus-unattested MW stratum is kośa/participle formations, not ghost-words
